evals.prompt: A blog archive page still looks too white. Update the website background system so the texture shows through behind the archive content, but the cards and typography remain readable.
evals.expected_output: The solution changes the page canvas or outer wrappers, keeps the cards readable, and mentions visual verification with a browser screenshot.
expectations: The solution changes a global canvas or section wrapper, not only card colors.
The solution keeps content readable while letting the background show through.
The response includes a browser screenshot or equivalent visual verification.

evals.prompt: A homepage section is hiding the background texture behind a white block. Fix the layering so the background remains visible, and do not make the text or images translucent.
evals.expected_output: The solution makes the wrapper transparent or moves the background to a proper layer, avoids opacity on content containers, and explains the stacking order used.
expectations: The solution uses a background layer or transparent wrapper instead of opacity on content.
The solution mentions stacking order, z-index, or isolation when relevant.
The content itself remains opaque and readable.

evals.prompt: Add a reusable background treatment for a website so future pages can use a soft texture canvas with readable cards and section-specific surfaces.
evals.expected_output: The solution defines a reusable background pattern or convention for the project and documents how future pages should use it.
expectations: The solution defines a reusable pattern for future pages.
The solution separates canvas, section, and card surfaces.
The response documents how to verify the result visually.
